back_translation: All you brothers and sisters, I myself was not able to speak with you as with people who are of the spirit; rather I had to speak with you as with those who are of the flesh — that is, as with infants in Christ.
I gave you milk to drink, not solid food, because you were not yet ready; and even at the present moment you are still not ready either.

Because you are still people who follow the flesh; for whenever there is jealousy and quarreling among you, are you not people who follow the flesh, walking in life according to the manner of mankind?
When one person says, "I am of Paul's faction," and another says, "I am of Apollos's faction," are you not merely human beings only?
Therefore, who is Apollos? And who is Paul? They are merely servants, through whom you came to believe — and to each one according to what the Lord has (royally) given.
I planted; Apollos watered; but God is the one who (royally) caused growth.
Therefore both the planter and the waterer are nothing at all, but God who (royally) causes growth — He is everything.
The planter and the waterer are one and the same; and each will receive his own reward according to his own labor.
Because we are co-workers of God; while you are God's cultivated field, you are God's building.

According to the grace of God which was given to me, I laid a foundation in the role of a master craftsman, and another person is building further upon it. But each one let him watch how he himself is building further upon it.
Because no one is able to lay another foundation besides the foundation which has already been laid — that is, Jesus Christ.
If anyone builds upon this foundation with gold, silver, gemstones, or with wood, grass, straw —
the work of each person will appear plainly, because that Day will reveal it; for that Day will come with fire, and the fire itself will prove what the work of each person is.
If the work of any person which he has built further upon endures, that person will receive a reward.
If the work of any person is completely consumed by fire, that person will incur loss; but he himself will receive salvation — though it will be like a person who escapes through the flames.
Do you not know that you yourselves are the temple of God, and that the Spirit of God (royally) dwells in you?
If anyone destroys the temple of God, God will (royally) destroy that one — because the temple of God is holy, and that temple is in fact you yourselves.
Let no one deceive himself. If anyone among you thinks that he himself has wisdom in this age, let him become a foolish person first, in order that he may become a wise person.
For the wisdom of this world is foolishness in the (royal) sight of God; for there is a written word: "He (royally) catches the wise in their own craftiness."
And another passage says: "The Lord (royally) knows the thoughts of the wise — that they are useless things."
Therefore, let no one boast in any human being; because all things are yours.
Whether Paul, Apollos, or Cephas; whether this world, life, or death; whether things that are present or things that will come in the future — all things are yours.
But you yourselves belong to Christ, and Christ Himself (royally) belongs to God.
